review_comment: can we get transparency from `unordered_set` with C++23 without our own hash/equal types?

As far as I know, the only thing that C++23 adds are more heterogenous overloads, but it remains opt-in so as to not break existing code. It would also not help us here, because just like C++, cppwinrt is also frozen in time and it's what would require the `is_transparent` attribute on its hash/equal_to structs ([here](https://github.com/microsoft/cppwinrt/blob/f9ec1986083a70d2f99d726b06b00a38cb2d1054/strings/base_std_hash.h#L37-L43)).
I suggest to move this into a global class that the logic part can be reused later in other places.
I know where that's coming from but in my experience (= decades) that's seldom useful.
(I should add that our development style is quite fluid. When the need for another policy check arises it'll not be a problem at all to move this code around.)
